inline_comments.message:
I assume this note was specific to VNF/PNF, if so might help clarifying it. 
VESCollector updates are more of configuration driven.

Why master branch? That doesn't appear to be a specific published version.
I think the use of branches is still being discussed in 3GPP including current SA5 meeting.

Patchset 2 is pushed, where this sentence is removed.
It`s a valid observation - this sentence is related to the VES Collector, and not to the VNF or PNF behavior.

Well, You`re correct, I am pointing to the master branch here, as there are no 3GPP release branches available yet. 

We have been urging our 3GPP delegates to reach an agreement on the branches reflecting specific 3GPP releases, I have a kind request to You, if You could reach out to E/// delegates, so a conclusion can be reached.

On the other hand, if we would point to a specific branch (once available), then we`d have to update this requirement each time, we change the contents of a built-in repo in a VES collector.

The target solution is based on a configMap, or external schema repo, where an operator is able to onboard specific openAPI descriptions, which she/he wants to use in her environment.
There is an ongoing work on a script and a procedure, how to create such a configMap, and how to attach it to any DCAE component (we plan to release it as part of Guilin).

In this context - do You have a proposal, how could this issue be approach in a fair way?

While this looks better than previous links, it is still not clear URLs are permanent and official. The repository description states 'Trial SA5 - data models' and the following warning is found on the main page:

Important note: This repository serves as a trial for 3GPP SA5 WG adoption. The current name, structure and address (i.e. URL) of the repository may change at the end of the trial period.
 
Indeed I believe there was an endorsed proposal (S5-204480) in the last SA5 meeting that included repository renaming in turn impacting URLs.

Looking at the YAML files themselves there appear to be a number of inconsistencies for notification definitions. However they do seem to match version of 28.532 approved in SA88.
The most serious thing I noticed yet is heartbeatNtf.yaml which probably will not work at all, due to notificationType field definition. (The root cause is really re-use of header from other MnS which seems like a bad idea.)

The code freeze for ONAP's Guilin release is this week.  We'll have some flexibility to resolve issues after that time, but when will the URLs for the schemas be finalized?  If this will remain beyond our Guilin timeline, then I would suggest we remove the links and refer to them by name.  However to do that, we we need to understand what ONAP has consumed in Guilin (i.e. has DCAE copied in versions of these schemas that could still be changed).  If DCAE has consumed schemas, then this presents a larger challenge unless the finalization of the schemas in 3GPP is imminent. Who can confirm both items?  1) when will these be published in 3GPP (final, static URLs) and 2) what has ONAP consumed?

With regards to the concern about heartbeatNtf.yaml - that sounds like it needs to be worked in 3GPP.  Who can take point on analyzing that issue and resolving it?

> when will the URLs for the schemas be finalized?  If this will remain beyond our Guilin timeline, then I would suggest we remove the links and refer to them by name.  However to do that, we we need to understand what ONAP has consumed in Guilin (i.e. has DCAE copied in versions of these schemas that could still be changed).  If DCAE has consumed schemas, then this presents a larger challenge unless the finalization of the schemas in 3GPP is imminent. Who can confirm both items?  1) when will these be published in 3GPP (final, static URLs) and 2) what has ONAP consumed?

Answering 1) How I understand this is that for now official publishing in 3GPP is via TS documents, so etsi forge is on best effort and changes like creation of permanent URLs are done via ETSI MCC, so only way to speed this is that all companies via they 3GPP delegates complain to 3GPP.

Answering 2) This is much simpler to answer.  The mentioned file are pre-loaded in DCA but can be change any time.

> With regards to the concern about heartbeatNtf.yaml - that sounds like it needs to be worked in 3GPP.  Who can take point on analyzing that issue and resolving it?

I can raise this issue to our delegates attention but still I don't think heartbeatNtf.yaml is used in ORAN and ORAN Alignment was main driver of this change.
==================================================================
I think that discussion is going away from the code change. The 2 requirements (R-408814, R-408815) are saying that you need to provide reference to exact data definition and later provide data which match this reference. Additionally second requirement inform that the linked definitions are "built-in". This information can be also provided as a pointer to 3GPP appendix or we can include whole files in requirements, we perceive those as less effective.
Do you see any issues in proposed requirements text which can be solve by changing the text?
https://git.onap.org/dcaegen2/collectors/ves/tree/etc/externalRepo/schema-map.json must be updated to reflect same

The issues Oskar raised are relevant and should be worked with 3GPP; they could be tracked as separate item and worked through ONAP 3GPP liason and/or TSC.

For Guilin release doc perspective, we could proceed if 1) Identify current 3GPP openAPI external references as draft 2) VNFREQ identified schema references must be consistent to what is supported by DCAE/vescollector  (path change required in dcae/ves as identified in earlier comment)

Whenever there is new revision/final schema from 3GPP, both VNFREQ documentation and DCAE VESCollector can be updated to reflect it. Based on the timing, this could be G point release or H release item.

Update of schema-map.json to reflect URLs from this file is ready here: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/collectors/ves/+/112424

Update of CSITs will be needed after schema-map.json update, MR is ready here:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/csit/+/112423

Given that DCAE has to be pre-configured with the schemas, and will have default schemas already onboarded.  I'm questioning these requirements. Based on a conversation with Vijay, DCAE will reject a stndDefined event that has an unknown schemaReference value.  That means we have mandatory requirements here - not recommendations.

The set of valid schemaReference field values MUST be a value registered with DCAE in advance in the schema-map.json file. 

I'd rather not have have to keep track of the valid values in two places so can we just link the schema-map.json and create requirements to the effect of the following...

* The VNF or PNF producing VES stndDefined domain events **MUST** only pass events that conform to schema references previously registered with DCAE otherwise the event will be rejected. By default, ONAP ships with support for schemas found here (link to schema-map.json).

* The VNF or PNF producing VES stndDefined domain events **MUST** set the schemaReference value to the publicUrl value in DCAE's schema-map.json file (link) that describes the data being sent in event.stndDefinedFields.data.

* The VNF or PNF Provider producing stndDefined events **MUST** coordinate with the operator to configure DCAE to accept any new event schemas prior to sending those events or the events will be rejected.


The VNF Requirements project exists to define what an NF needs to do to integrate with ONAP.  If we make this simply about what DCAE supports, then we can avoid the timing issues in 3GPP and more easily keep the VNF Requirements in sync with what ONAP supports.  DCAE can evolve what it supports and we don't have to keep the requirements in sync.
I think we need to have a meeting. We will present all use cases and we can together formulate VNF/PNF requirements.

Dear Vijay, 
Regarding point 2. I wanted to get consensus here first but Michal created already aligning MR.
Regarding point 1. I have no issue with this, I can prepare patch.
Shouldn't it be #/components/... in example? This doesn't look like valid JSON reference.

It doesn't need to. Implementation handles both #/components and /components. This is because ambiguity in Swagger documentation from one side it state that fragment need to be JSON Pointer so #/myEleemnt but in URL example they write 
"
The specific element of the document stored on the different server – $ref: 'http://path/to/your/resource.json#myElement'
"
Maybe it is typo but to be safe both works.
In case both alternatives are supported, can the requirement be clarifý this?
